en.mechanism_prose: Both substances suppress the brainstem reflexes that drive breathing. Combined, the effect compounds: respiratory depression that either substance alone might not cause becomes lethal. Sedation also stacks, making it harder to notice when breathing is failing.
warning_signs: Blue or gray lips, fingertips, or skin
first_aid: Place them in recovery position (on left side, top knee bent, head tilted)
If naloxone is available and an opioid is involved, administer it
Stay with them; rescue breathing if trained

en.mechanism_prose: Stimulants mask the dissociative effects of substances like ketamine, making it easy to redose ketamine to risky levels. Stimulant-driven hyperthermia and cardiovascular load compound with dissociation-driven loss of body awareness, raising injury and overheating risk.
warning_signs: Hot, dry skin or rising body temperature
Loss of motor control or coordination
first_aid: Move to a cool, quiet space
Hydrate slowly with water, not electrolyte rush
Do NOT give more of either substance
If unresponsive or breathing changes: call 911
